findings.claim: The spine (rblds_Spine.sh) already provides for free: recipe compilation (|-delimited step rows -> Build steps JSON), substitution-coverage validation, the full Build envelope (mason SA, TETHER pool, regime timeout, automapSubstitutions, CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY), submit, poll-to-completion, and buildStepOutputs slot decode. It owns NO kind knowledge. So any new kind that captures by submitting a Cloud Build of skopeo/docker/python steps reusing _RBGL_* substitutions reuses ALL of this with zero spine change.
findings.evidence: rblds_Spine.sh:19-31 (docstring: 'Owns NO kind knowledge'), :118-236 zrbld_spine_dispatch composes+submits+polls, :244-258 zrbld_spine_extract; capture-domain constants spine-owned at :195 (mason SA), :201-202 (TETHER pool, timeout)

findings.claim: The bole body (rbldb_Bole.sh) is genuinely thin: it owns only (a) a 2-row recipe naming rbgjl01/rbgjl02, (b) a jq substitutions blob of _RBGL_* registers, (c) host-side stamp mint + vessel-origin resolution, and (d) a per-member slot_n envelope extract loop. That is the per-kind NET-NEW surface a new vertical must rewrite. ~215 lines total, most of which is structure a new kind copies.
findings.evidence: rbldb_Bole.sh:55-58 (recipe rows), :62-90 (subs blob), :104-138 (extract loop), :143-214 (rbld_ensconce host orchestration: vessel resolve, single-origin enforce, stamp mint)

findings.claim: TOOL kind (verb fetter): payload is native layered OCI; member cardinality is N (6 tool images today: gcloud, docker, alpine, syft, binfmt, skopeo); fetch tool is docker pull (not skopeo); trust grade is recorded-at-acquisition (digest captured from RepoDigests after pull, NOT verified against a published checksum). The capture logic ALREADY EXISTS as rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh (docker pull/tag/push loop emitting tool->digest JSON). Absorbing it onto the spine is mostly mechanical: rename _RBGN_* registers to _RBGL_*, wrap each tool as a Lode member, add the vouch envelope/push step. SMALL-to-MEDIUM.
findings.evidence: rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh:22-29 (6-image MANIFEST), :43-54 (docker pull/tag/push), :47-48 (digest from RepoDigests = recorded-at-acquisition); rbfli_Inscribe.sh:31-126 (existing submit machinery the spine replaces); RBGC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED at rbgc_Constants.sh:232

findings.claim: RELIQUARY kind (verb conclave) is the SAME machinery as tool — it IS the current inscribe (rbfli_Inscribe.sh + rbgji01). 'reliquary' is an N-member date-cohort of exactly those tool images under one datestamp; the spec treats tool and reliquary as near-identical (RBSHR lists both as kind slots absorbing today's mirror/reliquary). The only divergence from tool is packaging cardinality framing (cohort-under-one-stamp vs per-tool member), and it carries the live 'inscribe' fork slated for cutover (GAR namespace rbi_rq -> rbi_ld). Tool and reliquary are the strongest candidates to GROUP — they share fetch tool (docker), trust grade (recorded), payload (native OCI), and source list.
findings.evidence: RBSHR-HorizonRoadmap.adoc:69-73 ('build-time tool images (today mirror/reliquary machinery)... reliquary demotes from top-level noun to a kind enum slot — the date-cohort semantic survives'); acronyms.md:49 reserves RBLDR 'reliquary/conclave'; rbfli_Inscribe.sh:142-144 reliquary datestamp = r+YYMMDDHHMMSS (same cohort form bole uses with kind-letter)

findings.claim: WSL kind (verb underpin) is the FARTHEST divergence from the bole/spine path. It fetches a rootfs tarball over plain HTTPS with a published SHA-256 — NOT an OCI registry pull. The spine's whole premise (skopeo/docker against a registry, _RBGL_* manifest tags, GAR->GAR retag, OCI digest as canonical identity) does not apply to a raw tarball. A new cloud step would need curl+sha256-verify (genuine verified-against-published, the only kind where the published-checksum branch is real), then wrap the opaque tarball as a FROM-scratch OCI artifact to land it in a Lode package. Member cardinality is 1 (one rootfs). This needs the most NET-NEW step logic and likely a new rbgjl0N step that shares almost nothing with rbgjl01-ensconce-capture. MEDIUM-to-LARGE, and structurally an outlier.
findings.evidence: RBSHR-HorizonRoadmap.adoc:75 ('wsl kind delivers a project-controlled WSL rootfs tar'); rbgjs-skopeo-fingerprint.sh requires ORIGIN as docker:// ref (registry-only); rbgjl01:99-113 skopeo copy --all + GAR->GAR retag assumes registry source; no existing rootfs-over-HTTPS+published-SHA fetch anywhere in Tools/rbk except the abandoned github path (rbhr_GithubRemote.sh:92-93 curl+tar, no Lode wiring)

findings.claim: PODVM kind (verb immure) is the LARGEST and most internally split. Payload is opaque blobs (CoreOS disk images / rootfs tarballs) wrapped as OCI — 'non-standard media types, custom disktype annotations, opaque blobs rather than composable layers'; standard docker/podman pull CANNOT retrieve them. Fetch tool is crane (manifest+blob navigation), not skopeo/docker. Cardinality is N across a fan-out: TWO quay families (machine-os-wsl 'mow' and machine-os 'mos') x multiple platform variants (arch+disktype). Trust grade is recorded-at-acquisition (provenance only reliably available at init time; brand-file captured post-hoc). The existing prototype (rbv_PodmanVM.sh) does crane manifest -> base64 entry decode -> per-platform crane blob -> repackage FROM-scratch -> push, and is GHCR-targeted and NEVER ported to GAR. Net-new body+cloud-step logic is large and shares little with bole beyond the spine envelope.
findings.evidence: RBSPV-PodmanVmSupplyChain.adoc:17 (opaque blobs, non-standard media types, crane/skopeo/oras below container abstraction), :23 (two families mow/mos with different build processes), :110 (blob-level extraction + multi-platform iteration); rbv_PodmanVM.sh:201-249 zrbv_process_image_type (crane manifest + base64 entry fan-out), :360-420 per-platform crane blob + FROM-scratch repackage, :21-26 GHCR not ported to GAR; acronyms.md:218 kind letters vw=podvm-wsl, vn=podvm-native (two distinct letters)

findings.claim: Trust-grade split is a real two-way axis, not per-kind cosmetic: bole and wsl can be verified-against-published (bole via canonical OCI digest, wsl via published SHA-256); tool, reliquary, and podvm are recorded-at-acquisition (digest captured from what was pulled, no upstream-published reference to check). Both constants already exist; the envelope already carries trust_grade as a substitution. So trust grade is data, not new code per kind.
findings.evidence: rbgc_Constants.sh:229-232 (two grades, comment: 'bole captures durable-upstream grade; podvm-* will carry recorded grade'); rbldb_Bole.sh:69 passes RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED; rbgjl01:128 trust_grade is a substitution register

findings.claim: All four remaining kinds reuse the host-side scaffolding bole established: stamp mint with kind-letter prefix (RBGC_LODE_KIND_* — only bole 'b' defined; t/r/w/vw/vn reserved but unwritten), the rbi_ld package-is-atomic-delete-unit layout, the rbi_vouch envelope + capture-file fact-multi pattern, and divine/banish/augur read verbs which are kind-agnostic. New constants per kind are trivial (a kind letter + reusing existing tag/trust constants).
findings.evidence: rbgc_Constants.sh:217-219 (kind letters reserved, only BOLE defined), :209 RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_LODES rbi_ld; rbldl_Lifecycle.sh:60 divine already enumerates by kind; acronyms.md:49 RBLDT/RBLDR/RBLDW/RBLDV reserved legend-only

input.recommendation_input:
Per-kind weight estimate (net-new body + cloud-step logic beyond pure spine reuse):

- tool (fetter): SMALL. Logic already exists (rbgji01 docker pull/tag/push). Mostly a rename/rewrap onto the spine + add vouch step. Native OCI, docker pull, recorded-at-acquisition, N members.
- reliquary (conclave): SMALL, and nearly identical to tool — same docker fetch, same recorded grade, same 6-image source, same datestamp-cohort. The divergence from tool is framing (cohort-under-one-stamp), not mechanism. Carries the inscribe fork to cut over.
- wsl (underpin): MEDIUM-LARGE. Structural outlier — HTTPS+published-SHA tarball fetch, NOT a registry pull. Needs a new cloud step (curl + sha256-verify + FROM-scratch wrap) that shares almost nothing with rbgjl01. It is, however, the one kind where verified-against-published is genuinely earned. Cardinality 1.
- podvm (immure): LARGE. Crane manifest/blob navigation, opaque non-layered blobs, two quay families (mow/mos) x platform fan-out, GHCR prototype never ported to GAR, two kind-letters (vw/vn). Most net-new logic by far.

Granularity recommendation:
1. GROUP tool + reliquary into ONE pace (or one pace with the second as a thin follow-on). They share fetch tool, trust grade, payload shape, and source list; reliquary is tool's date-cohort framing. Both absorb the same inscribe fork. Building them together avoids duplicating the docker-pull cloud step.
2. ONE pace for wsl. It diverges hard enough (HTTPS+published-SHA, new cloud step, opaque-tarball wrap) that it does not benefit from sharing a pace with the registry-pull kinds; but it is a single coherent vertical.
3. SPLIT podvm — recommend splitting along CAPTURE vs PLATFORM-SELECTION rather than native-vs-wsl. The crane manifest/blob capture machinery and the GAR-port of the FROM-scratch repackage are one body of work; the two-family + platform-variant fan-out (mow/mos selection, disktype/arch indexing, the vw/vn kind-letters) is a second, layerable concern. (Native-vs-wsl split is the weaker cut because both families share the identical crane capture path and differ only in which quay repo + variant set they enumerate.) At minimum podvm should be 2 paces; do not try to land it as one.

Net: roughly 3 build paces for the easy registry kinds + wsl (group tool/reliquary, separate wsl), and 2 paces for podvm = ~5 build paces, NOT one-per-kind (which would over-count the tool/reliquary near-duplication) and NOT a single grouped pace (which would bury the wsl/podvm divergence). The spine investment from BX pays off most for tool/reliquary (near-zero new machinery) and least for podvm (crane path is outside the spine's registry-pull assumptions but still rides the submit/poll envelope).

content.text:
I've completed the investigation and returned my findings via StructuredOutput. Summary of what I found by reading the real files:

The spine (`rblds_Spine.sh`) provides submit/poll/extract + the full Build envelope for free with zero kind knowledge. The bole body (`rbldb_Bole.sh`) is the thin per-kind surface (recipe rows + subs blob + extract loop) a new vertical must rewrite.

Per-kind divergence from the bole pilot:
- **tool** (fetter) — SMALL: native OCI, `docker pull`, recorded-at-acquisition, N members; logic already exists in `rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh`.
- **reliquary** (conclave) — SMALL and near-identical to tool (it IS today's inscribe, a date-cohort of the same images); strongest GROUP candidate.
- **wsl** (underpin) — MEDIUM-LARGE outlier: HTTPS + published-SHA-256 rootfs tarball, NOT a registry pull, so the skopeo/OCI-digest spine premise doesn't apply; needs a new cloud step. Only kind where verified-against-published is genuinely earned.
- **podvm** (immure) — LARGE: crane manifest/blob navigation, opaque non-layered blobs, two quay families (mow/mos) × platform fan-out, GHCR prototype never ported to GAR.

Recommendation: ~5 build paces — group tool+reliquary, one for wsl, split podvm into capture vs platform-selection — rather than one-per-kind or a single grouped pace.
